title: Comfort in affliction and God of all comfort
summary_text: Paul and Timothy write to Corinth with all saints; grace and peace. Blessed be God, Father of mercies, comfort of all comfort in affliction—comfort to comfort others. Sufferings of Christ abound; consolation abounds. Delivered from deadly peril; you help with prayer.
key_points: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies.
Who comforts us in all our affliction.
For as the sufferings of Christ abound to us, so our consolation also abounds.
He who delivered us out of so great a death.
evidence_phrases: the Father of mercies
who comforts us in all our affliction
the sufferings of Christ abound to us
whether we are afflicted, it is for your consolation
he who delivered us out of so great a death

title: Triumph in Christ and letter of recommendation
summary_text: Paul determined not to come again in sorrow; if cause joy, who is glad but he whom sorrowed? Forgiveness to offender. Triumph in Christ spreads fragrance of knowledge; letter of recommendation written on hearts by Spirit.
key_points: I determined this within myself, that I would not come to you again in sorrow.
If any has caused sorrow, he has caused sorrow not to me.
Now thanks be to God, who always leads us in triumph in Christ.
You are our letter, written in our hearts.
evidence_phrases: I determined not to come again to you in sorrow
if any has caused sorrow
thanks be to God, who always leads us in triumph
We are a sweet aroma of Christ
You are our letter, written in our hearts

title: New covenant glory; treasure in jars
summary_text: Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? You are our letter. Confidence through Christ toward God—not sufficient of ourselves. New covenant ministry of Spirit exceeds ministry of death in glory. Veil removed in Christ; beholding glory transformed. Treasure in earthen vessels.
key_points: Our sufficiency is from God.
He has made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant.
If the ministry of condemnation is glory, much more the ministry of righteousness exceeds in glory.
When Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart.
We have this treasure in clay vessels.
evidence_phrases: our sufficiency is from God
servants of a new covenant
the ministry of the Spirit
the glory of the old covenant
when Moses is read, a veil lies
we have this treasure in clay vessels

title: Affliction, hope, and inward renewal
summary_text: Therefore having this ministry, we don’t faint. Gospel veiled to perishing. Light shone in hearts for knowledge of God’s glory in Christ’s face. Outward man decays; inward renewed daily. Things unseen eternal.
key_points: Therefore seeing we have this ministry, we don’t faint.
The god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelieving.
It is God who said, ‘Light will shine out of darkness,’ who has shone in our hearts.
Our outward man is decaying, yet our inward man is renewed day by day.
The things which are seen are temporal; the things which are not seen are eternal.
evidence_phrases: we don’t faint
the god of this world has blinded
light will shine out of darkness
our outward man is decaying
light affliction works for us
the things which are not seen are eternal

title: Heavenly dwelling; reconciliation; ambassadors
summary_text: We know earthly tent dissolved—we have building from God. Groan to be clothed with heavenly dwelling. Made us new creation; ministry of reconciliation—God reconciling world in Christ. Ambassadors: be reconciled to God; he made him sin for us.
key_points: We know that if the earthly house of our tent is dissolved, we have a building from God.
Being always confident, knowing that while we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord.
If anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation.
We beg you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.
He made him who knew no sin to be sin on our behalf.
evidence_phrases: we have a building from God
we are absent from the Lord
if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation
the ministry of reconciliation
we are ambassadors therefore on behalf of Christ
He made him who knew no sin to be sin on our behalf

title: Holiness; unequal yoke; wide open heart
summary_text: Do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers; what fellowship light with darkness? Temple of living God—come out, be separate. Do not touch unclean; I will be Father to you. Open heart—widen yours also.
key_points: Don’t be unequally yoked with unbelievers.
We are a temple of the living God.
Come out from among them, and be separate.
Our mouth is open to you, Corinthians. Our heart is enlarged.
evidence_phrases: Don’t be unequally yoked with unbelievers
We are a temple of the living God
Come out from among them
I will be to you a Father
Our heart is enlarged

title: Godly sorrow and generous giving
summary_text: Paul caused sorrow by letter—godly sorrow leads to repentance without regret. Titus comforted; affection abounds. Macedonians gave beyond ability; you excel in faith, utterance, love—excel in grace also. Cheerful giver; God supplies seed for righteousness.
key_points: Godly sorrow produces repentance to salvation.
See what earnest care this very thing caused in you.
In most severe test, the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded to the riches of their liberality.
He who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly.
God loves a cheerful giver.
evidence_phrases: godly sorrow produces repentance
See what earnest care
their deep poverty abounded to the riches
He who sows sparingly
God is able to make all grace abound
God loves a cheerful giver

title: Grace of Christ and boasting in the Lord
summary_text: Grace of God in churches of Macedonia; first gave themselves to Lord. Christ was rich yet became poor. Eager willingness; God supplies. Titus—finish what was begun. Thanks for gift; odor of sweet smell. Boast in the Lord.
key_points: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor.
But as you abound in everything—in faith, utterance, knowledge.
Now finish doing it, as the readiness is there.
Thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift!
evidence_phrases: the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ
though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor
But as you abound in everything
Now finish doing it
Thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift

title: Paul’s defense and boasting in weakness
summary_text: Paul defends ministry—glory in conscience before God in holiness. Not peddling God’s word. If boasts, boasts in visions—thorn in flesh, power perfected in weakness. When weak, then strong.
key_points: We are not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully.
We commend ourselves as servants of God in great endurance.
There was given to me a thorn in the flesh.
Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses.
For when I am weak, then am I strong.
evidence_phrases: not handling the word of God deceitfully
commending ourselves as servants of God
a thorn in the flesh
My grace is sufficient for you
when I am weak, then am I strong

title: Weapons of warfare; examine yourselves
summary_text: Though walking in flesh, do not war according to flesh—divine weapons pull down strongholds. Casting down imaginations, bringing thoughts captive. Do not compare with commend self. Examine yourselves—Christ in you unless disqualified.
key_points: For though we walk in the flesh, we don’t wage war according to the flesh.
The weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh, but mighty before God.
We demolish arguments and every lofty thing that is raised up.
Examine your own selves, whether you are in the faith.
Jesus Christ is in you, unless perhaps you are disqualified.
evidence_phrases: we don’t wage war according to the flesh
the weapons of our warfare are mighty before God
bringing every thought into captivity
Examine your own selves
Jesus Christ is in you

title: False apostles and serpent craftiness
summary_text: Fear lest minds corrupted from simplicity toward Christ. Another Jesus, different spirit, different gospel. False apostles deceitful workers disguised as Christ’s servants. Boast as fool—sufferings, dangers, weaknesses.
key_points: I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy.
If he who comes preaches another Jesus, or if you receive a different spirit.
Such men are false apostles, deceitful workers.
Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
I have been in labor more abundantly.
evidence_phrases: I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy
another Jesus
false apostles, deceitful workers
Satan disguises himself as an angel of light
Are they servants of Christ?
I have been in labor more abundantly

title: Vision, thorn, and contentment in weakness
summary_text: Boasting necessary—man caught up to third heaven fourteen years ago; heard unspeakable words. Thorn given to keep humble; three times pleaded—my grace sufficient, power perfected in weakness. Gladly glory infirmities that Christ’s power may rest.
key_points: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third paradise.
There was given to me a thorn in the flesh, a messenger of Satan.
He has said to me, ‘My grace is sufficient for you.’
Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses.
For when I am weak, then am I strong.
evidence_phrases: caught up into the third heaven
a thorn in the flesh
My grace is sufficient for you
for power is perfected in weakness
when I am weak, then am I strong

title: Final warnings and Trinity blessing
summary_text: This third visit—every matter established by two or three witnesses. Examine yourselves; rejoice, aim for restoration, comfort, unity. Greet with holy kiss; holy kiss all saints. Grace of Lord Jesus, love of God, fellowship of Spirit be with you.
key_points: This is the third time I am coming to you.
Examine your own selves, whether you are in the faith.
Finally, brothers, rejoice. Be restored.
The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit, be with you all.
evidence_phrases: This is the third time I am coming to you
Every matter shall be established
Examine your own selves
Finally, brothers, rejoice
The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God
